---
title: |
  Materials Genomics<br>Unit 10: Multi-Fidelity and Physics-Informed Modeling
bibliography: ref.bib
author:
  - name: Prof. Dr. Philipp Pelz
    affiliation:
      - FAU Erlangen-Nürnberg
execute:
  eval: false
format:
  revealjs:
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    css: custom.css
    theme: custom.scss
    slide-number: c/t
    logo: "eclipse_logo_small.png"
    footer: "© Philipp Pelz - Materials Genomics"
---

## 01. Title: Multi-Fidelity and Physics-Informed Modeling
- Unit role in MG pipeline.

## 02. Learning outcomes
- Combine simulation and experiment intelligently.

## 03. Recap from Unit 9
- Active loop data acquisition context.

## 04. Fidelity concept
- Low-, mid-, high-fidelity sources.

## 05. Why multi-fidelity in materials
- Cost vs accuracy tradeoff.

## 06. Typical source hierarchy
- DFT, empirical, lab measurements.

## 07. Data alignment challenges
- Units, conditions, metadata.

## 08. Bias across fidelities
- Systematic discrepancy handling.

## 09. Co-kriging intuition
- Joint fidelity modeling.

## 10. Delta-learning approach
- Correct low-fidelity with high-fidelity residuals.

## 11. Transfer learning across fidelities
- Warm-start strategies.

## 12. Physics-informed features
- Invariants and constraints.

## 13. Physics-informed losses
- Penalty terms for plausibility.

## 14. Hybrid mechanistic-ML models
- Best-of-both-worlds setup.

## 15. Constraint-aware predictions
- Enforce feasible outputs.

## 16. Uncertainty in multi-fidelity models
- Source-aware confidence.

## 17. Active querying by fidelity
- Which source to query next.

## 18. Cost-aware acquisition
- Information per euro/hour.

## 19. Domain shift across labs/simulators
- Transfer robustness issues.

## 20. Calibration across fidelities
- Reliability by source.

## 21. Data curation protocol
- Provenance and versioning.

## 22. Missingness patterns
- Incomplete cross-fidelity labels.

## 23. Evaluation protocol
- Source-specific + aggregate metrics.

## 24. Ablation strategy
- Value of each fidelity stream.

## 25. Explainability under multi-fidelity
- Attribution consistency checks.

## 26. Failure mode #1
- Overtrusting low-fidelity trends.

## 27. Failure mode #2
- Physics constraints too rigid.

## 28. Failure mode #3
- Data harmonization leakage.

## 29. Mitigation checklist
- Robust integration workflow.

## 30. Case sketch: formation energy pipeline
- Multi-fidelity scaffold.

## 31. Case sketch: elasticity prediction
- Hybrid model scaffold.

## 32. Case sketch: catalyst screening
- Cost-aware query scaffold.

## 33. Visualization patterns
- Fidelity-stratified residual plots.

## 34. Reporting standards
- Document source provenance and costs.

## 35. Reproducibility requirements
- Deterministic splits and logs.

## 36. Human-in-the-loop review
- Expert checks for plausibility.

## 37. Integration with design loops
- Unit 11 generative bridge.

## 38. Exercise scaffold: build MF dataset
- Harmonize two fidelities.

## 39. Exercise scaffold: train baseline + delta model
- Compare paradigms.

## 40. Exercise scaffold: uncertainty analysis
- Fidelity-aware confidence checks.

## 41. Exercise scaffold: cost-aware query policy
- Source selection strategy.

## 42. Exercise scaffold: ablation report
- Quantify each source contribution.

## 43. Exam-oriented key statements
- High-yield concepts.

## 44. Summary table
- Methods vs assumptions.

## 45. Unit summary
- Core takeaways.

## 46. Reading assignment
- Generative materials models preview.

## 47. Transition preview
- Unit 11 roadmap.

## 48. References
- Source list.

## 49. Q&A buffer slide
- Discussion and synthesis.

## 50. Wrap-up checkpoint
- Position in overall course arc.

::: {#refs}
:::
